topic: Why Walking Slows Down After 60 — And What Your Body Is Trying to Tell You
niche: Educational biology of aging for adults age 50–75 (walking, balance, independence)
keyPoints:
Walking speed decline is an early biological signal, not just aging

The primary change is timing and coordination, not strength

The brain constantly evaluates stability and adjusts movement

Slower walking is compensation for reduced signal clarity

Early signs include hesitation, shorter steps, and careful movement

Changes appear in everyday situations (walking, turning, uneven ground)

Stairs expose these changes more clearly than flat walking

Early awareness allows intervention before major decline
callToAction: Subscribe for clear, biology-based videos on walking, balance, and staying independent longer
